metadata.notes: Voice rewrite — conversational walkthrough style matching Wingspan gold standard. Sources: official rulebook, Stonemaier FAQ, BGG forums, community strategy guides.

subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Board and Timers
- 1 main game board with 3 action rows — each row has multiple action spaces, and every space is split into 2 columns
- 3 sand timers with different durations:
  - **Black timer** (45 seconds) — cycles fast, gives small rewards
  - **Green timer** (2 minutes) — moderate actions, moderate wait
  - **Purple timer** (3 minutes) — slowest cycle, but the most powerful actions live here
- Each action space has a "before timer" column (left) and an "after timer" column (right), separated by a slot where the timer sits

#### Player Components (per player)
- 1 asymmetric character mat (double-sided: basic on one side, advanced on the other) with a unique ability and starting resources
- 2 worker pawns: 1 regular worker + 1 grande worker (the grande can share spaces with other players' workers)
- 4 track markers for Power, Prestige, Popularity, and Legendary Achievement
- 1 VP marker

#### Cards
- **Province cards** — your engine-building cards. They provide ongoing bonuses, resource production, or end-game scoring. You tuck them under your player board organized by action color.
- **Stratagem cards** — 4 unique cards per character side (40 total across all 10 character sides). These are one-time or ongoing rule-bending abilities you can play for free during the Council Phase.
- **Council Reward cards** — earned during the Rest Phase for track advancement.
- **Achievement cards** — bonus end-game scoring conditions.

#### Resources and Tokens
- Gold, vote, military strength, and prestige tokens
- Round marker (tracks rounds 1–4)
- Up to 10 influence tokens per player

#### Tracks
- 4 advancement tracks: Power, Prestige, Popularity, Legendary Achievement
- Each track has a **threshold line** — you must push all 4 markers past their thresholds into the "parchment" zone to be eligible to win

**Checkpoint:** Each player should have their character mat (basic side for first game), 2 workers, 4 track markers at starting positions, starting resources, and stratagem cards. The board should have the 3 timers in their starting positions, province cards in the display, and the round marker on Round 1.

subtopics.title: Game Structure & Core Loop
subtopics.content:
#### No Turns — Everyone Acts at Once
- **Simultaneous play** — All players act at the same time during the Council Phase
- **Real-time actions** — Place workers, flip timers, and collect rewards with no turn order
- **Theme** — You're powerful nobles vying to succeed the Timeless King as ruler of Dunya

#### How the Timers Work
- **3 sand timers** — One per action row on the board
- **Two columns per space** — Each action space is split into "before" (left) and "after" (right)
- **"Before" column (left)** — Place your worker here WHILE the timer is NOT on this row
- **"After" column (right)** — Workers slide here when the timer arrives and gets flipped; retrieve them to collect the action's reward

#### The Action Cycle — Step by Step
1. **Place a worker** on the "before" side of an action space on any row where the timer is NOT currently sitting.
2. **Wait** for the timer to run out and get flipped onto that row.
3. **Any player may flip** a timer once its sand runs out — flipping is voluntary and strategic. Nobody is forced to flip.
4. When flipped, ALL workers on the "before" side of that row slide to the "after" side.
5. **Retrieve your worker** from the "after" side to gain the action's reward.
6. Your worker is now free to be placed again.

#### Round Structure (4 Rounds Total)
- Each round has two phases:

**1. Council Phase (Real-Time)**
- **Duration** — Lasts until the purple timer has been flipped 3 times (roughly 9 minutes)
- **Simultaneous play** — Everyone places workers, flips timers, gains resources, and advances tracks all at once

**2. Rest Phase (Sequential)**
1. **Determine Privilege** — Based on who has the most vote tokens.
2. **Gain Council Reward cards** — In Privilege order (1st place: first pick + 2 VP advances; 2nd/3rd: 1 VP advance).
3. **Discard down** — To 2 province cards per color.
4. **Reset the board** — New cards and tokens.
5. **Reposition workers (optional)** — Move your workers to new starting positions.

#### Key Constraints
- Regular workers: only 1 per space (exception: the black section has unlimited capacity — any number of workers can share).
- Grande worker: can always be placed regardless of who else is there.
- You CANNOT place on a row where the timer has run out and is waiting to be flipped.
- You CANNOT retrieve from the "before" side — your worker must wait for the timer to push it to "after."
- A worker sitting on "after" blocks you from placing on the "before" side of that same space until you retrieve it.

**Checkpoint:** Before starting, make sure everyone understands the before/after column system and that flipping timers is voluntary. Walk through one cycle: place on "before" → timer flips → worker slides to "after" → retrieve for reward.

subtopics.title: Detailed Rules
subtopics.content:
#### Timer Flipping Rules
- **Any player may flip** — Once the sand runs out; you don't need a worker on that row
- **All workers slide** — When flipped, ALL workers on "before" simultaneously move to "after"
- **Flipping is voluntary** — Nobody is forced to flip; delaying is a legitimate strategic tool
- **Strategic delay** — Stall a flip to block opponents from retrieving, or buy time to place a worker
- **Cycle speeds** — Black (45s) cycles fastest with small rewards; Purple (3min) cycles slowest with the most powerful actions
- **Round end** — When the purple timer has been flipped 3 times total

#### Worker Placement Rules
- **Regular worker**: Can only go on unoccupied "before" spaces (exception: the black section has unlimited capacity — multiple workers can always share).
- **Grande worker**: Can be placed on any "before" space regardless of other workers present. However, a regular worker CANNOT be placed on a space already occupied by a grande worker (unless it's in the black section).
- You must retrieve a worker from "after" before you can place that same worker again.

#### Action Spaces
- **Gain resources** — Gold, votes, military strength, prestige tokens
- **Advance a track** — Pay required resources to push up Power, Prestige, or Popularity
- **Acquire province cards** — Draft from the shared display; tuck under your board by color; future activations of that color grant the province bonus too
- **Play stratagem cards** — Free action during Council Phase (no worker required); bend the rules in powerful ways
- **Convert resources** — Trade one type for another
- **Row power level** — Black = quick, small rewards; Green = moderate; Purple = most powerful but slowest to cycle

#### Province Cards (Your Engine)
- **Acquisition** — From the shared display via specific action spaces
- **Organization** — Tucked under your player board by action color
- **Activation** — Every time you take an action of that color, ALL province cards in that slot fire their bonuses
- **Cleanup** — Discard down to 2 per color during Rest Phase
- **Compounding value** — A card bought in Round 1 pays out ~12 times; bought in Round 4, maybe 3 times

#### Stratagem Cards
- **Starting hand** — 4 unique stratagems based on your character side
- **Free actions** — Play anytime during Council Phase at no cost
- **Effects** — Extra worker placement, free track advancement, resource windfalls, moving locked workers
- **Timing restrictions** — Some require specific triggers ("play when you flip a timer," "play during Rest Phase")
- **Council Reward cards** — Lightning bolt icons indicate one-time use

#### Character Asymmetry
- **10 playable characters** — 5 double-sided mats; basic sides play similarly, advanced sides diverge significantly
- **Asymmetric track lengths** — Each character has different distances to Power, Prestige, and Popularity thresholds
- **Characters** — Bolk ("The Champion" / "The Warmonger"), Licinia ("The Implacable"), Mesoat, Drenkhir, and Gambal ("The Briber")

#### Timeless Mode (Turn-Based Variant)
- **No real-time pressure** — Players take sequential turns: place a worker, retrieve a worker, or flip a timer
- **Same rules otherwise** — All mechanics stay the same
- **Recommended for first game** — Learn the systems without time stress

subtopics.title: Scoring & End Game
subtopics.content:
#### Game End
- After the Rest Phase of Round 4 — exactly 4 rounds, no early termination

#### The Win Condition: Cross All 4 Thresholds
- **4 tracks** — Power, Prestige, Popularity, Legendary Achievement; each has a threshold line dividing regular zone from "parchment" zone
- **Eligible to win** — ALL 4 markers must be past their thresholds into the parchment zone
- **1 player qualifies** — They win outright
- **Multiple players qualify** — Whoever is farthest past the thresholds in aggregate wins
- **Nobody qualifies** — Highest aggregate track position wins (rare with experienced players)
- **Central tension** — You MUST cross all 4 thresholds; maxing one track while neglecting another makes you ineligible

#### Additional Scoring
- **Province card bonuses** — End-game scoring based on track positions, resources, or conditions
- **Achievement card bonuses** — Bonus scoring conditions
- **In-game VP** — Already tracked during play
- **Character-specific bonuses** — From your character mat

#### Tiebreakers
1. **Lowest individual track position** — Higher is better.
2. **Highest Privilege** — Most votes accumulated.
3. **Most remaining resources** — Final tiebreak.

#### What to Expect
- **First game** — Focus entirely on crossing thresholds; don't worry about optimizing
- **Experienced play** — All 4 thresholds crossed with province bonuses and achievements stacked on top
- **Winner pattern** — Almost always someone who crossed all 4 thresholds; raw VP without threshold eligibility rarely wins

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Learn the Timer Rhythm First
- **Get comfortable with the cycle** — Place, wait, flip, retrieve; speed comes with practice
- **Start with black timer (45s)** — Fast cycles, frequent feedback, low stakes as your training wheel

#### Balance All 4 Tracks
- **Cross ALL 4 thresholds** — Ignoring any track is catastrophic for winning eligibility
- **Check your lagging track** — Always push the one that's falling behind
- **Study your character mat** — Each character has different track lengths; know which thresholds are close and which are far

#### Buy Province Cards Early
- **Compounding bonuses** — Province cards fire every time you take that action color
- **Round 1 purchase** — Fires across 4 full Council Phases
- **Round 4 purchase** — Fires maybe 3 times; front-load your engine

#### Don't Sleep on the Privilege Track
- **1st place advantage** — First pick of Council Rewards + 2 VP advances (vs. 1 VP for 2nd/3rd)
- **Compounds over 4 Rest Phases** — Accumulate vote tokens when you can

#### Use Your Grande Worker on Contested Spaces
- **Ignores occupancy limits** — Place it on high-value spaces where opponents are competing
- **Watch the purple row** — Think twice before parking the grande on a 3-minute space; it'll be locked for a long time

#### Fill Downtime with Stratagems
- **Free actions** — Play stratagem cards while waiting for timers to complete
- **Zero time cost** — Resource generation, worker repositioning, or track advancement

#### Keep Both Workers Active
- **Alternate workers** — While one waits for a timer, the other should be placing or retrieving
- **Never idle both** — Don't let both workers sit on "after" spaces simultaneously

#### Common Mistakes
- **Timer panic** — Rushing to place without thinking. Even in real-time, 2 seconds of thought saves wasted placements.
- **Track neglect** — Maxing Power while Prestige sits at zero. Your win eligibility is gated by your weakest track.
- **Forgetting to retrieve** — Workers on "after" are locked until you pick them up. Every idle moment is a wasted opportunity.
- **Ignoring the purple row** — 3-minute wait, yes, but the most powerful actions live there. Get a worker committed early each round.
- **Late engine building** — Province cards in Rounds 3–4 barely pay off. Build your engine in Rounds 1–2.
- **Not flipping timers strategically** — Flipping is voluntary. Sometimes flip early to retrieve your worker; sometimes delay to block opponents from retrieving theirs.

#### Timeless Mode for Learning
- **Same mechanics, no time pressure** — Play Timeless Mode first if real-time overwhelms you
- **Switch to real-time** — Once you've got the rhythm down

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### Setup
- Take your character mat (basic side for first game)
- Place 4 track markers at starting positions
- Take 2 workers — 1 regular, 1 grande
- Set starting resources per your character
- On the board: place 3 timers — black (45s), green (2min), purple (3min)

#### Round 1, Council Phase Begins
- **All timers start simultaneously** — Don't panic; breathe

#### Your First Move
- **Look at the black section (45s timer)** — Place your regular worker on a "before" space that gives resources you need
- **Fast cycle** — Within 45 seconds, someone flips it, your worker slides to "after"
- **Retrieve for reward** — You've completed one full cycle

#### While You Wait
- **Place your grande worker** — On a green or purple space; the grande goes anywhere regardless of other workers
- **Commit early to purple** — Powerful but slow; get your grande there early so it's ready when the timer flips

#### The Rhythm
- **Black timer (45s)** — Rapid small gains: resources, conversions; cycle your regular worker here
- **Green timer (2min)** — Moderate rewards
- **Purple timer (3min)** — Biggest payoff, longest wait; park your grande here

#### Track Advancement
- **Spend resources** — Advance Power, Prestige, Popularity, and Legendary Achievement tracks
- **Check your character mat** — Which thresholds are closest? Push those first
- **All 4 required** — You need ALL 4 past the line to win

#### Province Cards
- **Acquire from the display** — Tuck under your board by color
- **Engine building** — Every future activation of that color gives you the province bonus too

#### Round Ends
- **Purple timer flips 3rd time** — Council Phase is over
- **Rest Phase** — Determine Privilege (most votes), gain Council Reward cards, clean up, optionally reposition workers

#### Rounds 2–4
- **Same structure, growing engine** — Province cards compound, making each action more powerful
- **Push all 4 tracks** — Past their threshold lines
- **Use stratagems** — Free bonuses whenever you have a moment

#### Scoring
- **Cross all 4 thresholds** — Required to be eligible to win
- **Add bonuses** — Province bonuses + achievement bonuses + in-game VP
- **First game goal** — Cross at least 3 of 4 thresholds and get comfortable with the timer rhythm

subtopics.title: Advanced Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Timer Manipulation
- **Flipping is voluntary** — That's where advanced play lives
- **Delay flipping** — When your opponent has a worker on "before" and you don't, deny them the slide to "after"
- **Flip early** — When you need to retrieve before placing elsewhere
- **Purple timer control** — Controlling when it flips can give you an extra placement cycle that opponents miss

#### Worker Specialization
- **Grande worker** — Ignores occupancy limits; ideal for contested high-value spaces
- **Regular worker** — Stick to the black section (unlimited capacity, fast cycles) for reliable throughput
- **Default strong pattern** — Grande on purple + regular cycling black
- **When to break the pattern** — Only when a green-row province card is critical to your engine

#### Province Card Prioritization
- **Resource production cards** — Compound hardest; a gold-producing province in black (45s cycles) fires constantly
- **End-game VP cards** — Only worth grabbing if you're already pushing those tracks
- **Priority order** — Resource production > track advancement bonuses > end-game conditions

#### The 4-Threshold Map
- **Asymmetric track lengths** — Bolk's Power is short (easy) but Prestige is long (hard); Gambal's Popularity is easy but Legendary Achievement is steep
- **Pre-game planning** — Identify your character's 2 hardest thresholds before Round 1
- **Rounds 1–2** — Dedicate to your hardest thresholds
- **Rounds 3–4** — Top off easy thresholds and stack VP

#### Privilege Snowball
- **1st place advantage** — First pick of Council Rewards + 2 VP advances vs. 1 for everyone else
- **Over 4 rounds** — Up to an 8 VP swing plus consistently better card choices
- **Cheap votes** — If you can accumulate via province bonuses or black-row actions, the advantage compounds

#### Stratagem Timing
- **Don't fire all 4 in Round 1** — Plan when each has maximum impact
- **Bolk's free track advance** — Save for Round 3–4 when crossing a stubborn threshold
- **Gambal's bribery** — Strongest when opponents have resources worth stealing
- **Pre-game homework** — Read all 4 stratagems before the game and plan timing

#### Timeless Mode as Analysis Tool
- **Even for experienced players** — Study action space values, province card interactions, and track advancement costs
- **No time pressure** — Lets you analyze without the real-time stress
- **Direct improvement** — Insights from Timeless Mode improve your real-time decision speed

rulings: Timer flipping is voluntary — no rule forces any player to flip a timer. Strategic delay is legal and intended.
Workers are placed on rows where the timer is NOT present. Actions resolve when the timer returns (is flipped onto) that row.
The black section has unlimited worker capacity — any number of workers from any players can share a space there.
A grande worker CAN be placed on an occupied space. A regular worker CANNOT be placed on a space with a grande worker (except in the black section).
A round ends when the purple (3-minute) timer has been flipped 3 times, lasting approximately 9 minutes of real time.
Province cards gained during an action do NOT trigger during that same action — only on future activations of that color slot.
To be eligible to win, ALL 4 track markers (Power, Prestige, Popularity, Legendary Achievement) must be past their threshold lines into the parchment zone.
Each character has different track lengths — threshold distances vary per character, creating asymmetric difficulty profiles.
